elements.sent-2411-001: The probability current is a vital quantity in the Fokker-Planck description of stochastic processes.
elements.sent-2411-002: It characterizes nonequilibrium stationary states, appears in linear response calculation, and has been related to the entropy production and the heat flux.
elements.sent-2411-003: We recover and review the probability current in the Onsager-Machlup functional approach to Markov processes.
elements.sent-2411-004: We derive a self contained expression for the stationary probability current and the non-equilibrium fluctuation-dissipation theorem using field theoretical methods.
elements.sent-2411-005: The derived formulas are explicitly evaluated in the Ornstein-Uhlenbeck process of a harmonically bound particle in shear flow as exemplary analytic expressions.
elements.sent-2411-006: Our work closes a gap since it removes a missing link, i.e.~the probability current, in the supposed equivalence of the Fokker-Planck and the path-integral approach.
elements.sent-2411-007: Introduction The field of classical thermodynamics deals with the laws and principles governing the transformations of many-body systems, such as heat, work, or matter exchange with the environment.
elements.sent-2411-008: These general principles, like the first or second law of thermodynamics, refer to macroscopic quantities.
elements.sent-2411-009: Initially, thermodynamics focused on systems in equilibrium, justified by equilibrium statistical physics.
elements.sent-2411-010: A fundamental idea here is that microstates constituting a macrostate, described by state variables like energy or entropy, are distributed according to Gibbs-Boltzmann statistics.
elements.sent-2411-011: Close to equilibrium, linear response theory allows calculating transport properties through equilibrium correlation functions.
elements.sent-2411-012: At the heart of this is the fluctuation-dissipation theorem (FDT) and Onsager reciprocal relations \cite{Onsager_Reciprocal_Relations .}
elements.sent-2411-013: In the last three decades, the development of stochastic thermodynamics \cite{ oliveira_2020,Seifert_2012, Solr-871371 and the derivation of new exact relations valid in non-equilibrium systems \cite{Jarzynski_1997 have extended the validity of thermodynamics deep into the genuine non-equilibrium regime.}}
elements.sent-2411-014: In thermodynamic equilibrium, the forward and time-reversed processes occur with the same probability.
elements.sent-2411-015: In contrast, out of equilibrium, one of them is more likely than the other, indicating irreversibility and the production of entropy $S$.
elements.sent-2411-016: Many of these developments are rooted in stochastic processes.
elements.sent-2411-017: There are three commonly used descriptions of continuous stochastic dynamics.
elements.sent-2411-018: First, the Langevin equation which provides a microscopic equation of motion for individual particles.
elements.sent-2411-019: Second, the Fokker-Planck equation determining the evolution of the probability distribution.
elements.sent-2411-020: Both are linked via the Kramers-Moyal expansion \cite{KAMPEN2007,Risken .}
elements.sent-2411-021: Lastly, the path integral approach assigning a probabilistic weight to each trajectory.
elements.sent-2411-022: These three approaches are thought to be equivalent but complementary \cite{Altland_Simons_2010,Kamenev_2011 .}
elements.sent-2411-023: For example, the Fokker-Planck approach provides a probability current $\7J$, whose vanishing characterizes the equilibrium state and which is connected to the heat flux and the total entropy production \cite{oliveira_2020,Seifert_2012 .}
elements.sent-2411-024: Additionally, in the case of a non-equilibrium steady state (NESS), it determines the corrections to the equilibrium fluctuation-dissipation theorem \cite{Risken,Agarwal1972 .}
elements.sent-2411-025: The linear response out of equilibrium can not be understood in terms of entropy production alone.
elements.sent-2411-026: The contribution of the frenetic components were discussed in \cite{PhysRevLett.103.010602, C4CP04977B .}
elements.sent-2411-027: The correction to the entropic term of the equilibrium FDT has then be identified with the difference of entropic and frenetic effects of the perturbation \cite{Maes_2020 , which hence also constitutes the probability current \cite{Baiesi2009 .}}
elements.sent-2411-028: The richness of the literature and the insights in the origin of the probability current and non-equilibrium response theory make it quite surprising that no derivation of the probability current exists within the framework of the path integral approach, considering that the growing field of stochastic thermodynamics genuinely works at the level of single trajectories.
elements.sent-2411-029: Deriving $\7J$ from a path integral expression for the transition probability might significantly impact this field.
elements.sent-2411-030: Additionally, it would close a gap in the often-stated equivalence of the different approaches.
elements.sent-2411-031: In this work, we provide this missing piece.
elements.sent-2411-032: Building on the identification of entropic and frenetic components of an applied perturbation, we derive the probability current in a general linear response theory within the framework of the Onsager-Machlup stochastic path integral.
elements.sent-2411-033: It turns out to be essential to pay close attention to the discretization of the stochastic integral.
elements.sent-2411-034: The Stratonovich convention allows the identification of entropic and frenetic effects of a perturbation.
elements.sent-2411-035: However, we argue that an anti-It\^o discretization leads to the probability current as it allows expressing the difference of frenetic and entropic components as a state variable of the transition probability.
elements.sent-2411-036: Deriving this result, we restrict ourselves to Markov processes, where the transition probability does not depend on the history of the system, which is hence said to have no memory.
elements.sent-2411-037: Additionally, we assume ergodicity so that the dependence on the initial state also vanishes.
elements.sent-2411-038: In Section \ref{sec_FP , we quickly review the Fokker-Planck description of Markov processes and state the probability current.}
elements.sent-2411-039: After introducing the path-integral formalism in section \ref{sec_OM , we present results of the linear response calculation in and out of equilibrium in section \ref{sec_Lin_Rep .}}
elements.sent-2411-040: We derive equivalent equations in the path integral formalism and thus re-obtain the expression for the current.
elements.sent-2411-041: Our calculations implicitly exploit that both approaches are based on the same infinitesimal transition probabilities, which are the fundamental objects in both cases.
elements.sent-2411-042: In section \ref{sec_Equivalence , we build on this fact and show that the correlation functions of finitely many dynamical variables in both theoretical frameworks are the same.}
